title: Webull Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Webull pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Webull Pricing Overview

Webull offers a genuinely commission-free trading platform with a freemium pricing model: a zero-cost Standard tier for all retail traders, and a $3.99/month optional upgrade (Webull Premium) that adds advanced tools. Founded in 2017 and publicly traded on NASDAQ under ticker BULL, Webull has built its business on accessibility—there's no account minimum, no inactivity fees, and no required paid tier. If you want to trade stocks, ETFs, options, and crypto without paying anything, Webull delivers that. If you want institutional-grade data and execution tools, Premium adds them for less than the cost of a coffee per month.

All Webull Plans Compared

Feature Standard (Free) Webull Premium
Monthly Cost $0 $3.99/mo ($40/year annual)
Stock Trading ✓ Commission-free ✓ Commission-free
Options Trading ✓ Commission-free ✓ Commission-free
Futures Trading ✓ Available ✓ Available
Crypto Trading (50+ assets) ✓ Available ✓ Available
Extended Hours (4AM–8PM ET) ✓ Included ✓ Included
Paper Trading (Ranked #1) ✓ Included ✓ Included
56+ Technical Indicators ✓ Included ✓ Included
Order Flow Analysis ✓ Included ✓ Included
Replay Mode ✓ Included ✓ Included
Level 2 Quotes & Data Feeds — ✓ Premium add-on
Lower Margin Rates Standard rates ✓ Reduced rates
AI-Powered Analysis ✓ Included ✓ Included
News Feed & Alerts ✓ Included ✓ Included
Education & Resources ✓ Included ✓ Included

The stark reality: the free tier is feature-complete for most retail traders. Paper trading, technical charting, crypto, options, and order flow analysis come standard. Premium ($3.99/month) layers in Level 2 quotes and reduced margin rates—meaningful upgrades for active traders but not required to get started.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Webull's entire Standard tier is free with no trial period and no time limit. You open an account, fund it with any amount (no minimum), and trade immediately. The free tier includes:

  • Commission-free trading: stocks, ETFs, options, futures, and 50+ cryptocurrencies
  • Paper trading: risk-free simulation with virtual cash, ranked #1 by StockBrokers.com for 2026
  • 56+ technical indicators: the core charting toolkit for technical analysis
  • Order flow analysis: transparency into volume and execution pressure
  • Replay Mode: backtest strategies by replaying historical market data
  • Mobile-first platform: full-featured trading app for iOS and Android
  • Extended hours: trade from 4AM to 8PM ET, plus overnight trading on select securities
  • AI analysis and news: real-time market insights and alert customization

The free plan is worth starting with. Unlike many brokers that gate essential features behind paywalls, Webull doesn't. You can paper trade indefinitely, learn the platform, and decide if Premium fits your workflow before spending a dime.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Webull advertises "commission-free" trading, and that's accurate—but several layers of cost exist below the surface:

1. Payment for Order Flow (PFOF)

Webull generates approximately 50% of its revenue from payment for order flow, where market makers compensate the broker for routing trades to them. This can impact execution quality. While Webull's execution is generally competitive, traders prioritizing millisecond-level fills may find alternatives preferable.

2. Margin Rates

Interest accrues on margin balances. Standard tier margin rates are higher than Premium; upgrading to Premium reduces these rates, creating indirect pressure to upgrade if you use margin frequently.

3. Crypto Withdrawal Restrictions

Webull does not allow transfers of cryptocurrency to external wallets. If you accumulate crypto on the platform, you're locked in—you can only sell. This creates a cost if you want to move holdings to hardware wallets or other exchanges.

4. Account Types Unavailable

Webull does not offer SEP IRAs, inherited IRAs, trust accounts, or custodial accounts (529s, UTMAs). If you need these account types, you'll incur the opportunity cost of using a different broker for tax-advantaged investing.

5. Data Feeds & Subscriptions

The Standard tier includes charting tools, but advanced data feeds (Level 2 quotes, real-time institutional data) require Premium. For casual traders, this is negligible; for active day traders, it's a material upgrade cost at $3.99/month.

6. No Fee for Inactivity or Maintenance

To Webull's credit, there are no hidden account fees, inactivity fees, or minimum balance requirements—a rarity among brokers.

Webull Pricing vs Competitors

Webull's pricing sits at the aggressive end of the retail broker spectrum. Here's how it compares:

Broker Base Cost Premium Tier Level 2 / Advanced Data
Webull Free $3.99/mo ($40/yr) Included in Premium
Robinhood Free Robinhood Gold: $9.99/mo Included in Gold
E*TRADE Free None (all features free) Free
TD Ameritrade Free None (all features free) Free

Webull vs Robinhood Gold: Webull Premium ($40/year) is 60% cheaper than Robinhood Gold ($119.88/year). Both unlock Level 2 quotes and reduced margin rates. Webull's value proposition is significantly better on price alone.

Webull vs E*TRADE and TD Ameritrade: Both are free with no paid tier required. However, they target institutional traders and retirement accounts more than mobile-first day traders. Webull's platform feels faster on mobile and includes paper trading ranked #1 by independent reviewers. The trade-off: E*TRADE and TD Ameritrade have stronger customer support and broader account types.

For price-sensitive active traders, Webull wins decisively. For retirement and tax-advantaged investing, E*TRADE and TD Ameritrade are more complete.

Is Webull Worth the Price?

Standard (Free) for:

  • New traders learning the market via paper trading—the free tier's #1-ranked simulation removes barrier to entry
  • Commission-conscious swing traders holding positions overnight using extended hours—4AM–8PM ET access costs nothing
  • Crypto traders wanting exposure to 50+ assets without paying taker fees (though withdrawals off-platform are blocked)
  • Mobile traders who prioritize app speed and UI over institutional-grade data feeds

Premium ($3.99/month) for:

  • Day traders and scalpers who use margin and benefit from Level 2 quotes to understand bid-ask microstructure
  • High-volume traders whose margin costs drop enough to offset the $40/year Premium subscription
  • Traders who want institutional-grade transparency without paying the $99–$199/month cost of platforms like ThinkOrSwim or Lightspeed

When to Upgrade: If you're placing 5+ trades per day using margin, Premium's lower rates will pay for itself. If you're swing trading without margin, Premium adds little value—the free tier has everything you need.

Value vs Risk: Webull's 4.2/5 rating reflects strong platform quality, but customer support is ranked last among major brokers (0.92/10 score)—wait times exceed 10 minutes. If you need help fast during market hours, this is a real cost in the form of trading downtime. Also, roughly half of Webull's revenue comes from payment for order flow, which may subtly disadvantage execution compared to brokers routing to multiple venues. These are acceptable trade-offs for cost-conscious traders; they're dealbreakers for professionals who prioritize execution and support.

How to Save on Webull

1. Annual Billing for Premium

Webull Premium is $3.99/month (charged monthly), or $40/year if paid annually. Paying annually saves 17% ($9.88 per year). For most traders, this is negligible, but every dollar counts.

2. Skip Premium Entirely for the First 3 Months

Start with the free Standard tier. Use paper trading to validate your strategy with zero cost. Upgrade only after confirming you're a consistent trader; most new accounts go inactive within 90 days.

3. Avoid Margin Until Necessary

Margin interest compounds fast. If you can trade on cash balance alone, you dodge the interest cost entirely. Premium's lower margin rates become valuable only if you're regularly carrying margin balances.

4. Use Crypto Wallets Outside Webull

If you intend to hold cryptocurrency long-term, buy on Webull (no fees), then immediately transfer to a hardware wallet—except Webull blocks transfers. Instead, use Coinbase, Kraken, or another exchange that allows wallet transfers for your crypto holdings. Use Webull only for trading spot crypto, not storing it.

5. Refer a Friend (if available)

Webull occasionally runs referral bonuses—check your account for active referral links. Both parties typically receive cash or free stock. This is a one-time savings, not recurring.

6. Paper Trade First

Webull's paper trading tool is ranked best-in-class. Before risking real capital, practice for 2–4 weeks on virtual cash. This avoids the costly mistake of trading with real money before you're ready—statistically, 85% of day traders lose money in their first year.

The reality: there are no hidden discounts or loopholes to reduce Webull's pricing below what's listed. The platform's affordability comes from its business model (payment for order flow), not from behind-the-scenes deals. That transparency is worth something.
